ddls:
CREATE TABLE "changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the change was made */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., add, update, release) */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the change */,
  "incompatible" TEXT /* Indicates if the change introduces any incompatibilities */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the change */,
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the release associated with the change */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number associated with the change */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the change */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the change */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_changes_release_id FOREIGN KEY ("release_id") REFERENCES releases ("release_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_changes_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* Parent category ID */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "releases" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release */,
  "major_version" INTEGER /* Major version number of the release */,
  "minor_version" INTEGER /* Minor version number of the release */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date of the release */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Detailed notes about the release, including major changes and features */,
  "release_status" TEXT /* Status of the release */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, project manager, quality assurance) */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* ID of the team the user belongs to */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions for the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change being logged */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was logged */,
  "log_action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "log_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the log entry */,
  "log_status" TEXT /* Status of the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_logs_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES changes ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_change_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dependencies" (
  "dependency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dependency */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change that depends on another change */,
  "dependent_change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change being depended on */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dependency */,
  "dependency_type" TEXT /* Type of dependency (e.g., prerequisite, concurrent) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dependency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dependencies_dependent_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("dependent_change_id") REFERENCES changes ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dependencies_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES changes ("change_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "issues" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each issue */,
  "change_id" INTEGER /* ID of the change that introduced the issue */,
  "issue_type" TEXT /* Type of issue (e.g., bug, compatibility issue) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the issue */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the issue (e.g., open, in progress, resolved) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to resolve the issue */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the issue was created */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the issue was resolved */,
  "issue_priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the issue (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "issue_resolution" TEXT /* Description of the issue resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_issues_change_id FOREIGN KEY ("change_id") REFERENCES changes ("change_id")
)
